diagnosis.final_diagnosis: Systemic-onset juvenile idiopathic arthritis (SoJIA) with cardiac tamponade
diagnosis.diagnostic_reasoning: The diagnosis was confirmed based on the presence of persistent fever, maculopapular rash, splenomegaly, lymphadenopathy, inflammatory syndrome, and pericardial effusion leading to cardiac tamponade. The patient's condition worsened despite initial treatment with nonsteroidal anti-inflammatory drugs and high-dose methylprednisolone, but improved dramatically after administration of anakinra, supporting the diagnosis of SoJIA.
